title: ONCP — Open Network for Content Platform
tagline: Telecom didn't expand — it vertically integrated the entire digital stack. ONCP is a DPI-layer response: protocol over platform, built underneath the duopoly, not against it
summary: A personal vision for redefining digital distribution in India — born from watching a father's cable business being structurally bypassed, not outcompeted. Since Jio's disruption, telcos now own all five layers: network, content, device, application, and data. LCOs have been reduced to dumb pipes with zero leverage. ONCP proposes a five-layer open protocol stack (content delivery, shared infra, device neutrality, identity + billing, edge AI) that turns LCOs from legacy operators into sovereign infrastructure nodes — the same way UPI turned banks into rails, not gatekeepers. Inspired by ONDC and UPI. Not a startup. Not a deck. A direction I intend to spend years working toward.
